executive_briefing.summary: System stress at ELEVATED → HIGH with zero bullish asset class scores: consumer confidence at a GFC-trough (55.5), Hormuz supply disruption embedding a $15–20 oil premium, and private credit gate risk the most underpriced systemic trigger in the model.
executive_briefing.one_number_to_watch: VIX 24.15 with CDX at a 9-month high while S&P sits within 5% of all-time highs — this combination preceded drawdowns in 2007, 2015, and 2022 every single time.
executive_briefing.one_indicator_lying: Nominal M2 growth (+4.88%). Real M2 vs. Core PCE is +1.78% and narrowing. The market is using the nominal figure to justify compressed risk premia.

outlook.label: BEARISH
outlook.rationale: All eight asset classes are in negative territory. No bullish scores. Stagflation regime confirmed at high conviction. Base case 55% slow burn; 25% deflationary bust; 20% fast cascade.
outlook.zero_bullish_note: The absence of a single positive forward outlook is itself the signal.

360_intelligence.headline: Hormuz: Zero Commercial Crossings as of March 14
360_intelligence.summary: ~400 vessels holding in Gulf of Oman. Fujairah alternative hub hit by drone strike. De facto Iranian control of access. Binary outcome: resolution = sell energy/buy duration; 30+ day extension = Brent $120–150.

360_intelligence.headline: Private Credit Gate Risk: 40% Probability by End Q2 2026
360_intelligence.summary: Blackstone, Blue Owl, BlackRock redemptions approaching 5% gate threshold. Morgan Stanley projects defaults rising 5.6% → 8%. Gate events move from private to public rapidly, triggering CLO/IG credit contagion.

360_intelligence.headline: Real M2 Policy Trap — Q3 2026 Trigger Window
360_intelligence.summary: Nominal M2 +4.88% masks Real M2 +1.78% (vs Core PCE) and approaching zero (vs PPI pipeline). Fed cannot cut, hold, or raise without worsening the trap. When Real M2 last turned negative, banking stress followed within 6–12 months.

360_intelligence.headline: JGB Yield Spiral: 30yr at 3.55% — BoJ Pain Threshold Breached
360_intelligence.summary: ~¥400T in life insurer/pension holdings at risk. Emergency bond-buying → yen depreciation → FX swap demand → Treasury basis trade stress. No modern precedent for coordinated JGB/Treasury stress event.

360_intelligence.headline: CDX/VIX Divergence: 4 for 4 Historical Drawdown Signal
360_intelligence.summary: CDX investment-grade at 9-month high while S&P within 5% of ATH. Every prior instance (2007, 2015, 2022) preceded a material drawdown. Not yet a sell signal — a confirmed reduce-and-wait signal.

flags.title: Hyperscaler capex trajectory reinforces AI Infrastructure Debt indicator
flags.body: $750B hyperscaler capex in 2026 with energy infrastructure lock-in (nuclear PPAs, two-tier power grid) creating a durable moat by 2028. Directly reinforces ai_infra_debt (ELEVATED, capex/revenue 45–57%). Anthropic IPO at $380B and Shield AI at $12.7B consistent with private credit late-cycle stress. AI capex is both a debt amplifier (2026–2027 risk) and potential deflationary force (2028–2030 payoff) — two effects on different timescales.

flags.title: AI labour displacement beginning to show in entry-level employment data
flags.body: Anthropic research documents 14% drop in job entry rates for workers aged 22–25 in AI-exposed occupations. Consistent with Macro Monitor jobless_claims divergence (February payrolls -92K vs. claims 205K). If AI displacement is contributing to the payrolls revision, the Fed's labour market anchor is weaker than headline data suggests. Not yet confirmed as dominant driver — emerging signal only.

flags.title: EU CSRD Phase 2 creates mandatory climate risk disclosure for financial institutions
flags.body: EU CSRD Phase 2 entered force 1 January 2026; first reporting obligations in 2027. Double materiality requirements expose EU financial institutions to mandatory climate risk disclosure for the first time. Structural cost amplifier for European banks and insurers already under G-SIB capital pressure. Adds to cost base of institutions tracked as systemic risk vectors. Significant on 12–24 month horizon.

flags.title: Sudan economic warfare indicator above baseline — reinforces EM Sovereign Distress; Myanmar/Iran jet fuel is a secondary Hormuz signal
flags.body: Strategic Conflict Monitor flags Sudan's Economic Warfare indicator (I4) above baseline alongside Rhetoric, Diplomatic, and Displacement — four-indicator simultaneous breach. Sudan is a Fragile Five EM, part of em_sovereign_distress composite. Quad mediation framework non-operational since September 2025 removes primary de-escalation mechanism. Additionally, Myanmar junta sustaining air campaigns via Iranian jet fuel delivery is a secondary Hormuz-linked supply chain signal consistent with oil_supply_shock WARNING flag.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: Current FIMI operations focused on electoral and political interference. No identified macro-financial market manipulation or central bank credibility attacks. Hungary election (April 12) is a potential EM stress catalyst for HUF/Hungarian sovereign spreads if Tisza victory triggers Russian economic retaliation — speculative at this stage.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: Hungary election crisis and Georgia civil society capture are political stress signals not yet translated into financial contagion vectors. Hungary election (April 12) watch: Tisza leads Fidesz by 23 points. A Tisza victory could trigger Russian economic retaliation against Hungary — potential EM stress signal for HUF. Monitor post-election.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: NATO 20% European defence spending increase is a fiscal signal — elevated defence budgets add to sovereign debt trajectories in European economies. Structural rather than acute. Reviewed: 31 March 2026.
